items.title: Science vs. religion?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Science and religions have been at odds for centuries, but are they inherently incompatible? We ask philosopher William Lane Craig, who is also a evangelical Christian, why science is so important.</article>

items.title: Denis Noble - Philosophy of Extended Evolutionary Synthesis
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

What is the extended evolutionary synthesis (EES) and why better? Includes multilevel selection, transgenerational epigenetic inheritance, niche construction, evolvability, and ideas from evolutionary developmental biology. Why is EES controversial?

Denis Noble CBE FRS FMedSci MAE is a British physiologist and biologist who held the Burdon Sanderson Chair of Cardiovascular Physiology at the University of Oxford from 1984 to 2004 and was appointed professor emeritus and co-director of computational physiology.

Stacie Friend is a Reader in Philosophy at the University of Edinburgh. She previously taught at Birkbeck from 2005-2007 and again from 2014-2023, having taught in between at Heythrop College, University of London. Her research is at the intersection of aesthetics, language and mind, especially as these pertain to our engagement with works of fiction.

<article>Robert Kawrence Kuhn answers fan questions on theories of consciousness, cognitive computation, theism, determinism, and more.

items.title: Can the eye solve the hard problem of consciousness?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Does our vision give us unique insights into a physicalist theory of consciousness? Professor Naotsugu Tsuchiya shares his research on how the cornea, the clear dome shaped part of our eye, can be used with Integrated Information Theory, one of the currently leading theories of consciousness to tackle the hard problem of consciousness.</article>

items.title: Bruce Hood - What Causes Religious Belief?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Get access to over 5,000 videos with a free Closer To Truth membership: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Bruce MacFarlane Hood is a Canadian-born British experimental psychologist and philosopher who specialises in developmental cognitive neuroscience.

Bruce MacFarlane Hood is a Canadian-born British experimental psychologist and philosopher who specialises in developmental cognitive neuroscience.

items.title: Massimo Pigliucci - Philosophy of Transhumanism & Artificial Intelligence (AI)
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Contribute what you can to support Closer To Truth: https://closertotruth.com/donate/

Humanity’s future is now framed by artificial intelligence (AI) and increasing interventions in the human body to cure and to enhance, i.e., transhumanism. Considering the stakes, and the dangers, a philosophical perspective is imperative.

Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

Massimo Pigliucci is an American philosopher and biologist who is professor of philosophy at the City College of New York, former co-host of the Rationally Speaking Podcast, and former editor in chief for the online magazine Scientia Salon.

Closer To Truth, hosted by Robert Lawrence Kuhn and directed by Peter Getzels, presents the world’s greatest thinkers exploring humanity’s deepest questions. Discover fundamental issues of existence. items.title: Iain McGilchrist - Idealism: Arguing Pro and Con?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow us on X for videos, articles, and polls on life's biggest questions: https://x.com/CloserToTruth

Idealism is the theory that all reality is consciousness and only consciousness, and that what we perceive to be the physical world is either a manifestation of consciousness or derived from it. Either way, fundamental reality is pure consciousness. What are the arguments for and against Idealism (like the common sense of the physical)?

Sign up for a free Closer To Truth membership to receive exclusives like early access to new episodes: https://closertotruth.com/register/

Iain McGilchrist is a former Fellow of All Souls College, Oxford, an associate Fellow of Green Templeton College, Oxford, a Fellow of the Royal College of Psychiatrists, a Fellow of the Royal Society of Arts, a Consultant Emeritus of the Bethlem and Maudsley Hospital, London, a former research Fellow in Neuroimaging at Johns Hopkins University Medical School, Baltimore, and a former Fellow of the Institute of Advanced Studies in Stellenbosch.

items.title: Quayshawn Spencer - How are Human Beings Unique?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Like us on Facebook for daily videos, updates, announcements, and much more: https://shorturl.at/tak4l

What is “human” nature? While human beings are animals, we believe we are special, radically different from other animals. Why? What traits are unique to humans? Are we sure? How to explore human nature from a biological and evolutionary perspective?

Wear your support for the show with a Closer To Truth merchandise purchase: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

Quayshawn Spencer is the Robert S. Blank Presidential Associate Professor of Philosophy and Undergraduate Chair at the University of Pennsylvania.

items.title: Jimo Borjigin on Neural Signatures of Near-Death Consciousness | Closer To Truth Chats
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Hear the latest ideas, including: mechanisms of quantum consciousness; the meaning of AI and transhuman consciousness; how psi, altered states, and near-death experiences affect consciousness.

Watch more CTT Chats here: https://t.ly/jJI7e

Jimo Borjigin is an Associate Professor of Molecular & Integrative Physiology as well as an Associate Professor of Neurology at the University of Michigan. Borjigin received her Ph.D. in Neuroscience at The Johns Hopkins Medical Institution in Baltimore, MD. Her research focuses on the use of neural correlates of covert consciousness in the dying brain.

Support the show with a tax-deductible donation of any amount:  https://shorturl.at/OnyRq</article>

items.title: Charles Harper Jr. - Did God Create Multiple Universes?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for daily videos, updates, and announcements: https://www.instagram.com/closertotruth/

What is the extent of God’s creation? If there are multiple universes, did God create each of them? If an infinite number of universes, did God create all of them? Theists may be conflicted: the centrality of human beings in this one universe, and, for Christians, the incarnation on Earth, versus the magnification of God with multiple universes.

Make a tax-deductible donation of any amount to help support Closer To Truth continue making content like this: https://shorturl.at/OnyRq

Charles L. Harper Jr. is former Executive Director and Senior Vice President of the John Templeton Foundation.

items.title: Anil Seth - What Living Things are Conscious?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Shop the Closer To Truth merch store for items like hoodies, T-shirts, mugs, and more: https://bit.ly/3P2ogje

We know we humans are conscious and we strongly suspect higher animals are as well: for example, primates, dogs, cetaceans, whales and dolphins. But how far down the phylogenetic scale does consciousness go? Do fish feel pain? Do insects have awareness? Do bacteria sense? What are the implications?

Anil Seth is Professor of Cognitive and Computational Neuroscience at the University of Sussex, where he is also Director of the Sussex Centre for Consciousness Science. Seth is also Co-Director of the Canadian Institute for Advanced Research (CIFAR) Program on Brain, Mind, and Consciousness. Seth’s mission is to advance the science of consciousness, and to use its insights for the benefit of society, technology, and medicine.

items.title: Ivette Fuentes on quantum gravity
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>After her talk at the Science of Consciousness Festival, quantum physicist Ivette Fuentes joins us to discuss Roger Penrose's work on the interplay between general relativity and quantum mechanics.

Is the answer to quantize gravity or gravitize quantum mechanics? She shares her thoughts.</article>

items.title: Ask Me Anything with Robert Lawrence Kuhn (Part I)
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Robert Lawrence Kuhn answers viewer questions like how to deal with the overwhelming amount of information, data and theories on the Internet; Schoppenhauer’s understanding of nature and reality; is consciousness physical; and are you any closer to truth than when the show started?

items.title: Stacie Friend - How Art Affects the Self
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content:
<article>Subscribe to the Closer To Truth podcast on Apple, Spotify, or wherever you get your podcasts: https://shorturl.at/mtJP4

There is increasing evidence in the field of neuroscience that art enhances brain function by impacting brain wave patterns, emotions, and the nervous system. But more abstractly, does art have the power to affect the self? That is, a person’s beliefs, thoughts, feelings, identity. What impact does art have on the individual consuming it?

Follow Closer To Truth on Instagram for updates, announcements, and videos: https://shorturl.at/EzYo9

Stacie Friend is a Reader in Philosophy at the University of Edinburgh. She previously taught at Birkbeck from 2005-2007 and again from 2014-2023, having taught in between at Heythrop College, University of London. Her research is at the intersection of aesthetics, language and mind, especially as these pertain to our engagement with works of fiction.

items.title: Is Robert Lawrence Kuhn really closer to truth?
items.author: Closer To Truth
items.content: <article>Last year we recorded our first ever "Ask Me Anything" with host and co-creator Robert Lawrence Kuhn. The very first question: Now that he's been doing Closer to Truth for over 20 years, has he gotten any closer to "truth"? #shorts</article>
